source: InterNACHI SoP §3.6 — active leaks must be reported

visible_defects.defect: Water droplets and staining on p-trap and cabinet floor

vision_analysis.context_notes: PVC p-trap. Pooled water on the cabinet floor liner, water stains on the wood.

input.transcript: Under the kitchen sink — active drip from the p-trap. There's standing water on the cabinet floor and the wood is stained. Been going on for a while.

expected.rationale: Active leak with evidence of duration (staining, wood damage). SoP explicitly requires reporting active leaks. Major because it's causing secondary damage.

visible_defects.defect: Toilet rocks slightly when pressure is applied; visible gap at floor

vision_analysis.context_notes: Toilet base shows small gap between porcelain and tile floor. No visible water staining on floor or ceiling below.

input.transcript: Hall bath toilet is loose at the base, rocks a little. Not leaking right now but the wax ring is probably going. Should get it re-set.

expected.rationale: SoP explicitly lists 'loose connections to the floor' as a defect to report. No active leak yet so minor_repair, not major.

source: InterNACHI SoP §3.6 — water flow at two fixtures

visible_defects.defect: Reduced flow when sink and shower run simultaneously

vision_analysis.context_notes: Single-family home, ran sink and shower in same bathroom.

input.transcript: Pressure drops noticeably in this bathroom when the sink and the shower run at the same time. Could be supply line size, could be aging galvanized. Worth monitoring.

expected.rationale: SoP says report flow deficiencies at two fixtures. Inspector said 'monitor' explicitly. Could indicate galvanized pipe nearing end of life but not yet a failure.

vision_analysis.primary_subject: water heater TPR valve and discharge tube

visible_defects.defect: TPR discharge tube terminates ~24 inches above floor, not within 6 inches

vision_analysis.context_notes: Discharge tube is copper, terminates well above the floor drain.

input.transcript: Water heater TPR discharge tube ends about two feet off the floor. Should be within six inches of the floor. Code issue.

expected.rationale: TPR discharge improperly terminated = if valve opens, scalding water sprays at occupant height. Code violation. Major repair.
